title: Seven Chances

evidence.note: Published 1925; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — Seven Chances (1925), full film

editorial: Seven Chances is a 1925 American silent comedy directed by and starring Buster Keaton, in which a man must marry by a deadline to inherit a fortune, released through Metro-Goldwyn Pictures and famous for its climactic boulder chase. As a 1925 publication, the film print is in the US public domain by expiry of the 95-year term. Any restored or re-scored editions are assessed separately.
